home.heroSubtitle: A curated hub of AI-made games. Play, explore, and publish yours with a simple PR.
home.value1Body: Every game here is submitted via Pull Request — transparent, reviewable, and versioned.
home.value2Body: A clean card gallery, progressive loading, and direct links to playable builds.
home.value3Body: Ship prototypes, jam entries, or polished releases. Add a cover, tags, and a short tagline — done.
home.footerNote: No gatekeeping. Just good vibes and playable builds.

games.title: Games
games.subtitle_desc: Discover AI-made games across genres — bite-sized experiments, jam builds, and polished releases.
games.empty: No games yet. Be the first to submit one.
games.externalHint: External links open in a new tab.

about.title: About VibeGam.ing
about.p1: VibeGam.ing is a living gallery of games made with the help of AI — from weird one-night prototypes to surprisingly polished worlds.
about.p2: We believe the best way to understand a new creative medium is to play it. That’s why we keep submissions simple: edit one file, open a PR, and your game becomes part of the collection.
about.c2: Clear credit and links to creators
about.submitBody: Want your game featured? Add it to src/content/games.ts and open a Pull Request.
about.faq1Q: Do I need to be a pro developer?
about.faq1A: No! We welcome first-time creators, jam submissions, and simple prototypes.
about.faq2Q: Does it have to be 100% AI generated?
about.faq2A: No. We showcase games where AI played a significant role in code, art, audio, or design.
about.faq3Q: How do I update my game?
about.faq3A: Just submit another PR with the changes to games.ts.
about.faq4Q: Can I submit non-game AI experiments?
about.faq4A: Yes! Interactive art, music toys, and visualizers are all welcome.
about.contact: Questions or partnerships: open an issue on GitHub.
